steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says hoards were found "earlier" than the twenty-first century, and the blank must complete the example introduced by "For example." The table provides discovery dates. The correct choice needs to show at least one hoard found before 2000, and the data cited must be accurate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the Balline Hoard was found in 1868.’: This gives only one hoard, but stating a single date does not clearly establish the point that hoards were found "earlier" in a way that contrasts with a 21st-century pattern. More importantly, it names only one item when the table shows multiple pre-2000 finds. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the Ardagh Hoard, Balline Hoard, and Carrick-on-Suir Hoard have all been found since 1868.’: "Since 1868" spans into the twenty-first century and does not confirm the finds were pre-2000 — this fails to support the "found earlier" claim. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the Ardagh Hoard and Balline Hoard were both found before 2000.’: Both hoards appear in the table with discovery dates before 2000, directly confirming that meaningful finds happened before twenty-first-century technology. This is the specific, accurate, and complete example the sentence needs. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Balline Hoard and Carrick-on-Suir Hoard were both discovered in the 1900s.’: The Carrick-on-Suir Hoard's date in the table does not confirm it was discovered in the 1900s — this makes a claim the data does not support. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
